name: Skel-Tex
description_short: Skel-Tex Credit Union is a small, member-owned financial institution in Skellytown, Texas, established in 1964, serving 600+ members with savings accounts, loans, and basic banking services.
description_long: Skel-Tex Credit Union was established in 1964 and is headquartered in Skellytown, Texas, operating as a state-chartered, not-for-profit financial cooperative. With over 60 years of history, it has built its reputation as a community-focused institution serving the residents of the Skellytown area and surrounding Texas communities. The credit union stewards $5.9 million in assets and maintains a lean operational structure with over 3 dedicated employees. Skel-Tex offers a comprehensive suite of basic financial products including regular savings accounts, share draft accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs with 14+ accounts), money market accounts, auto loans (new and used vehicles), personal loans, mortgage loans, wire transfers, IRA/KEOGH retirement accounts, and online bill pay services. Members also have access to online banking, a mobile app for iOS and Android, online loan payments, and financial calculators. Current loan rates include unsecured credit cards at 0.00% APR, new vehicle loans at 10.58% APR, used vehicle loans at 11.08% APR, and other unsecured loans/lines of credit at 14.40% APR. As a not-for-profit credit union, Skel-Tex distinguishes itself by offering competitive rates and lower fees compared to for-profit banks. The institution operates with a limited branch network (1 branch, 0 ATMs) but provides modern digital banking services including online banking and mobile applications. The organization emphasizes personalized service with direct phone contact available at (806) 848-2413 and membership eligibility based on Texas residency or specified community ties. However, Skel-Tex operates as a very small, geographically limited institution with only $5.9 million in assets and a single branch location. The lack of ATM access and minimal branch network means members may face inconvenience for cash withdrawals or in-person services. The institution currently has no customer reviews available on the platform, limiting transparency about member experiences. Prospective members should verify current membership eligibility requirements and service availability, as the credit union's small size may limit product customization or rapid service expansion.
pros: Not-for-profit structure with competitive loan rates and lower fees than for-profit banks
Established 60-year track record since 1964 with stable community presence
Modern digital banking options including mobile app for iOS and Android and online banking
Diverse loan offerings including mortgages, auto loans (new and used), and personal loans
Unsecured credit card product with 0.00% APR
Direct member service contact at (806) 848-2413 with personalized support
cons: Very small institution with only $5.9 million in assets, limiting financial stability and product innovation compared to larger credit unions
Single branch location in Skellytown, TX with zero ATMs, creating significant accessibility challenges for members
No customer reviews available on the platform, making it difficult for prospective members to assess service quality or member satisfaction
Limited geographic reach with membership likely restricted to Texas residents, reducing options for those relocating
Minimal online presence and information availability suggests potential gaps in digital service maturity
best_for: Texas residents in or near Skellytown seeking community-based banking with personalized service
Members prioritizing lower fees and not-for-profit structure over branch convenience
Small business owners or individuals needing basic lending products from a local institution
Long-term members already established with the institution seeking to maintain community banking relationships
diagnosis: Skel-Tex Credit Union is best for Texas-based individuals who value community banking, not-for-profit principles, and personalized service over branch convenience and modern digital features. The primary caveat is the institution's very small size ($5.9M assets, 1 branch, 0 ATMs), which significantly limits accessibility and may raise questions about long-term viability and competitive product offerings compared to larger regional credit unions.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
